author: jsign
content:
Sharing some experimental spec benchmark filling. 

This requires a precise definition of what I mean here:
1. These fixtures were filled directly from `execution-specs` with usual `uv run fill` but using an exernal t8n tool (geth in this case). So, are not generated by external tool like `zkevm-benchmark-workload` repo by replaying in Reth and “filling the gaps” — we want external tools to disappear for this.
2. This is done for `tests-zkevm@v0.4.1` so no new assumptions about spec changes, you can try it on your current guest programs.
3. `tests-zkevm@v0.4.1` is based on `bal@v7.2.0` which should fit `glamsterdam-devnet-5`. I used `glamsterdam-devnet-5` from geth to fill with t8n. Some of the benchmarks filling failed (~70 cases from ~1000 ish). But this is prob not a geth problem, more like a EEST benchmark fixture assertion problem reg 8037 implications on “expected gas assertions” we have in benchmarks. These 70 cases are “out” of the filling — we prob need some “policy” in EEST reg benchmark correct support in devnets (maybe gets better in galmsterdam-devnet-7, so we can wait).

Doing an [early sharing if somebody wants to run them](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1IV3AaljEhz5ZwOyRM29-OuuJEqsD8oVG/view?usp=sharing). They are filled only to 60M (but we can fill to a higher or lower gas limit as usual). Everything here relies on the stability of geth reg `statelessInputBytes` compliance with `tests-zkevm@v0.4.1` specs, so if some run fails, it can be because of geth or your guest program (since we can’t use specs to fill heavy benchs).

All experimental: you can give it a spin and let me know. If it isn’t that bad, I can put this filling as part of the release pipeline so we also publish them as part of releases. But want to do a temp check first.
